name: Sound Advice
description_short: Sound Advice buys and sells precious metals, jewelry, and consumer electronics including smartphones and computers in Baltimore. They offer cash for unwanted valuables with competitive pricing.
description_long:
Sound Advice operates as a pawn shop and precious metals buyer in Baltimore, Maryland, located at 217 E Baltimore St between Guilford Ave/South St and N Calvert St/S Calvert St. The business is licensed (License # 2232-1) and serves customers seeking to convert valuable items into immediate cash.

The company operates a dual-service model. On one side, they purchase precious metals and jewelry, explicitly emphasizing competitive pricing ("We pay the most and won't be beat!"). They claim to educate customers about precious metal values and negotiate prices to customer satisfaction. On the other side, they buy and sell newer consumer electronics, specifically mentioning iPhones, Android smartphones, high-end computers, and DSLR cameras. They promise fair pricing and customer satisfaction on electronics transactions.

Sound Advice distinguishes itself through their stated commitment to competitive precious metals pricing and their dual focus on both jewelry/metals and electronics. They operate with limited hours (closed weekends, open 10:30 AM on weekdays, with extended hours Monday-Thursday until 5:30 PM and Friday until 3:30 PM) and provide direct phone contact at (410) 244-5100. The business also lists an additional cell phone contact (515-999-5675) for inquiries.

As a pawn shop, Sound Advice provides a straightforward liquidity option for customers with valuable items but offers no credit-building, financial counseling, or long-term financial solutions. The business model depends entirely on customer possession of valuable goods and provides only immediate cash conversion without financing instruments or repayment flexibility.
address: Down The Stairs, 217 E Baltimore St, Baltimore, MD 21202

company_info.headquarters: Down The Stairs, 217 E Baltimore St, Baltimore, MD 21202

pros: Advertises competitive precious metals pricing with explicit promise not to be undercut
Dual service model covering both precious metals/jewelry AND consumer electronics
Licensed operation (License # 2232-1) for transparency and legitimacy
Direct phone contact available for price inquiries before visiting
Specializes in high-value electronics (DSLRs, iPhones, Android phones, computers)
Extended weekday hours (until 5:30 PM Mon-Thu) for working customers
Explicit focus on customer education about precious metal values
cons: No weekend hours (closed Saturday and Sunday) limits accessibility
Typical pawn shop model offers below-market rates despite competitive claims
No financing or credit-building components—purely transactional
Limited location (single Baltimore storefront) restricts geographic access
No mention of appraisal guarantees or price-lock commitments
best_for: Customers with unused jewelry, precious metals, or high-end electronics needing immediate cash
People seeking to liquidate valuable items without selling through online marketplaces
Individuals preferring in-person negotiation over standardized buyback quotes
diagnosis: Sound Advice is best for customers with valuable physical assets (jewelry, electronics) who need immediate liquidity and prefer in-person transactions over online selling. The critical caveat is that pawn shops typically offer below-market rates despite competitive advertising, and this business provides no credit-building, financing, or long-term financial solutions.
services: Gold and jewelry appraisal and valuation
Price negotiation and customer education on precious metals
